description: Read SAM/BAM alignment files as a table. Supports headerless SAM files with reference_lengths parameter, glob patterns, and parallel processing.
Read FASTA/FASTQ sequence files as a table. Supports single-end and paired-end reads, glob patterns, gzip compression, and quality score offset conversion.
Read SFF (Standard Flowgram Format) files from 454/Roche sequencing. Returns read_fastx-compatible schema with optional quality/adapter trimming.
Read sequences from SAM/BAM alignment files in read_fastx-compatible schema. Extracts sequence and quality data from alignments.
Read BIOM (Biological Observation Matrix) format files. Returns sparse COO format with sample_id, feature_id, and value columns.
Read GFF3 annotation files. Parses attributes column into a SQL MAP for easy querying.
example: SELECT type, attributes['gene'] AS gene FROM read_gff('annotations.gff');

description: Fetch GenBank metadata from NCBI by accession number. Returns organism, length, taxonomy_id, and other metadata.
Fetch FASTA sequences from NCBI by accession number. Returns data in read_fastx-compatible schema.
Fetch feature annotations from NCBI by accession number. Returns data in read_gff-compatible schema.
Read jplace phylogenetic placement files. Returns best placement per fragment with edge_num, likelihood, and like_weight_ratio.
example: SELECT fragment, edge_num, like_weight_ratio FROM read_jplace('placements.jplace');

description: Read mzML mass spectrometry files. Returns one row per spectrum with metadata, m/z arrays, and intensity arrays.
Read mzXML mass spectrometry files. Returns same schema as read_mzml for cross-format compatibility.
Read chromatogram data from mzML files. Returns chromatogram metadata with time and intensity arrays.
Read Newick phylogenetic tree files. Returns one row per node with parent-child relationships and branch lengths.
example: SELECT name, branch_length FROM read_newick('tree.nwk') WHERE is_tip;

description: Execute MassQL queries against mass spectrometry data. Supports conditions (MS1MZ, MS2PROD, MS2PREC, MS2NL), aggregations, and X/Y-variable pattern matching.
example: SELECT * FROM massql('QUERY scaninfo(MS2DATA) WHERE MS2PROD=167.09', 'sample.mzML');

description: Align query sequences to subject sequences using minimap2. Supports pre-built indexes for 10-30x performance improvement.
Align query sequences against multiple pre-built minimap2 index shards in parallel. Reads are routed to specific shards via a mapping table.
Build and save a minimap2 index to disk for reuse with align_minimap2.
Align query sequences to subject sequences using Bowtie2. Optimized for short reads.
Align query sequences against multiple pre-built Bowtie2 index shards in parallel. Reads are routed to specific shards via a mapping table.
Classify sequences against a RYpe minimizer index. Returns bucket assignments with confidence scores. Supports paired-end and negative index filtering.
Extract deduplicated forward and reverse complement minimizer hash sets from sequences.
Extract minimizer hashes with positional information for both forward and reverse complement strands.
Compute log-ratio classification between two RYpe indices (numerator vs denominator). Returns per-read log-ratio scores.
Test if read is paired (SAM flag 0x1).
Test if read is properly paired (SAM flag 0x2).
Test if read is unmapped (SAM flag 0x4).
example: SELECT * FROM read_alignments('a.bam') WHERE NOT alignment_is_unmapped(flags);

description: Test if mate is unmapped (SAM flag 0x8).
Test if read is on reverse strand (SAM flag 0x10).
Test if mate is on reverse strand (SAM flag 0x20).
Test if read is first in pair (SAM flag 0x40).
Test if read is second in pair (SAM flag 0x80).
Test if alignment is secondary (SAM flag 0x100).
example: SELECT * FROM read_alignments('a.bam') WHERE NOT alignment_is_secondary(flags);

description: Test if alignment is primary (neither secondary nor supplementary).
Test if read failed QC (SAM flag 0x200).
example: SELECT * FROM read_alignments('a.bam') WHERE NOT alignment_is_qc_failed(flags);

description: Test if read is PCR/optical duplicate (SAM flag 0x400).
example: SELECT * FROM read_alignments('a.bam') WHERE NOT alignment_is_duplicate(flags);

description: Test if alignment is supplementary (SAM flag 0x800).
example: SELECT * FROM read_alignments('a.bam') WHERE NOT alignment_is_supplementary(flags);

description: Calculate sequence identity between read and reference. Supports gap_compressed (default), gap_excluded, and blast methods.
example: SELECT alignment_seq_identity(cigar, tag_nm, tag_md) FROM read_alignments('a.bam');

description: Calculate total query length from a CIGAR string. Optionally includes hard-clipped bases.
Calculate proportion of query bases covered by alignment. Supports 'aligned' (default) and 'mapped' types.
Compute gap-affine pairwise alignment score using WFA2. Returns integer score (0 = identical).
Compute pairwise alignment score and extended CIGAR string using WFA2. Returns STRUCT(score, cigar).
Compute pairwise alignment with score, CIGAR, and aligned sequences using WFA2. Returns STRUCT(score, cigar, query_aligned, subject_aligned).
Calculate reverse complement of DNA sequences. Supports IUPAC ambiguity codes and preserves case.
Calculate reverse complement of RNA sequences. Supports IUPAC ambiguity codes and preserves case.
Convert DNA sequences with IUPAC codes to regular expression patterns for pattern matching.
Convert RNA sequences with IUPAC codes to regular expression patterns for pattern matching.
Compute monoisotopic mass in Daltons from a chemical formula string.
Convert a MassQL query string to its generated SQL for debugging and inspection.
Aggregate function that merges overlapping genomic intervals into a minimal set of non-overlapping intervals.
example: SELECT reference, compress_intervals(position, stop_position) FROM read_alignments('a.bam') GROUP BY reference;

description: Compute the fractional mass (mass defect) of an m/z value.
Check if observed m/z is within absolute tolerance (Da) of target m/z.
Check if observed m/z is within relative tolerance (ppm) of target m/z.
Check if an m/z value's mass defect falls within a specified range.
Unnest mz_array and intensity_array into per-peak rows with normalized intensities.
Re-aggregate peak-level data to one row per scan with summary statistics.
Compute total intensity per spectrum.
Return distinct spectrum indices.
Return distinct precursor m/z values.
Return maximum intensity per spectrum.
Filter mzml_peaks output to MS level 1 only.
Filter mzml_peaks output to MS level 2 only.
Return MS1 peaks from parent scans of filtered MS2 peaks.
Return MS2 peaks from child scans of filtered MS1 peaks.
MS1 peaks from parent scans of MS2 spectra containing a product ion at target m/z.
MS2 peaks from child scans of MS1 spectra containing a peak at target m/z.
MS1 peaks from parent scans of MS2 spectra with matching precursor m/z.
MS2 peaks from scans with a product ion and whose parent MS1 has a peak at target m/z.
Filter peaks near a target m/z within tolerance.
Filter peaks with neutral loss near a target value within tolerance.
MS2 peaks from scans that do NOT contain a product ion at target m/z.
MS1 peaks from scans that do NOT contain a peak at target m/z.
MS2 peaks from scans whose precursor is NOT near target m/z.
Find spectra with peaks at variable X and X+delta (pattern matching).
Find spectra with peaks at X, X+delta2, and X+delta3.
Find spectra with peaks at X plus a list of N offsets.
Offset pair matching with X constrained to a min/max range.
Find MS2 spectra where precursor minus delta matches a product ion.
Find MS2 spectra with precursor mass defect in a specified range.
Find MS2 spectra whose precursor matches an MS1 peak.
Match spectra containing N-of-M target m/z values (cardinality constraint).
Find peak pairs at X and 2*(X - formula_mass) for formula-based pattern matching.
Match spectra with isotope patterns at specified offsets and intensity ratios.
Max-normalize an intensity array by base peak intensity.
TIC-normalize an intensity array by total ion current.
Parse a GFF3 attribute string (semicolon-delimited key=value pairs) into a DuckDB MAP.
Compute Woltka OGU counts over alignment data for multiple samples. Accounts for multi-mapped reads.
Compute Woltka OGU counts over alignment data for a single sample. Accounts for multi-mapped reads.
Compute genome coverage from alignment data. Compresses intervals per contig, maps to genomes, and returns proportion covered.
Check if the bowtie2 binary is available in PATH.
Return the MIINT extension version string.
Return a table of dependency library versions (htslib, minimap2, WFA2-lib, etc.).
